allies.rationale_text: Commitment bias is the behavioral output of cognitive dissonance resolution — the motivated drive to maintain consistency with prior public commitments is powered by the dissonance generated between the commitment and any evidence that challenges it.
allies.source_quote: Commitment bias is the behavioral output of cognitive dissonance resolution: the motivated drive to maintain consistency with prior public commitments is powered by the dissonance it generates.

allies.rationale_text: Confirmation bias is one of cognitive dissonance's primary resolution tools — seeking out confirming evidence and minimizing disconfirming evidence reduces the dissonance that would arise if contradictory evidence were weighted honestly.
allies.source_quote: Confirmation bias is one of cognitive dissonance's primary resolution tools: seeking confirming evidence reduces the dissonance that would arise if contradictory evidence were weighted honestly.

antagonists.rationale_text: Intellectual humility is the direct antagonist to dissonance-motivated belief revision — it is the disposition to hold beliefs provisionally and update genuinely rather than defensively; dissonance drives belief revision toward comfort while intellectual humility drives it toward accuracy.
antagonists.source_quote: Intellectual humility is the direct antagonist: it is the disposition to hold beliefs provisionally and update genuinely rather than defensively.

antagonists.rationale_text: Critical thinking requires engaging honestly with evidence that conflicts with current beliefs — exactly the cognitive operation that dissonance-motivated reasoning is designed to prevent.
antagonists.source_quote: Critical thinking requires engaging honestly with evidence that conflicts with current beliefs — exactly what dissonance-motivated reasoning prevents.

antagonists.rationale_text: The pre-mortem is a deliberate structural intervention against dissonance — by requiring failure scenario imagination before commitment is locked in, it prevents the post-commitment dissonance that would otherwise motivate belief revision.
antagonists.source_quote: The pre-mortem prevents the post-commitment dissonance that would otherwise motivate belief revision by requiring failure scenario imagination before commitment crystallizes.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Cognitive-dissonance conflicts with intellectual-humility when the motivated drive to eliminate belief inconsistency converts contrary evidence into a threat to be defended against rather than information to be genuinely updated on.
structured_tensions.source_quote: cognitive-dissonance vs intellectual-humility: cognitive-dissonance conflicts with intellectual-humility (intellectual humility) when the motivated drive to eliminate belief inconsistency converts contrary evidence into a threat.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Cognitive-dissonance conflicts with commitment-bias when escalating commitment to a failing course of action is powered by the need to reduce the dissonance generated by sunk investment.
structured_tensions.source_quote: cognitive-dissonance vs commitment-bias: cognitive-dissonance conflicts with commitment-bias (commitment bias) when escalating commitment is powered by the need to reduce sunk-investment dissonance.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Cognitive-dissonance conflicts with critical-thinking when the discomfort of inconsistency converts honest evidence evaluation into motivated advocacy for the pre-committed position.
structured_tensions.source_quote: cognitive-dissonance vs critical-thinking: cognitive-dissonance conflicts with critical-thinking (critical thinking) when the discomfort of inconsistency converts honest evidence evaluation into motivated advocacy.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Cognitive-dissonance conflicts with pre-mortem when post-commitment dissonance suppresses the failure-scenario imagination that the pre-mortem technique depends on.
structured_tensions.source_quote: cognitive-dissonance vs pre-mortem: cognitive-dissonance conflicts with pre-mortem (pre-mortem) when post-commitment dissonance suppresses the failure-scenario imagination.

curation_notes.summary: Cognitive dissonance Wave 3 curated from new source markdown. Key allies: commitment-bias (behavioral output of CD), confirmation-bias (primary resolution tool). Key antagonists: intellectual-humility (direct antidote), critical-thinking, premortem. Four structured tensions.
